renders_corpus: EQO/1 — corpus releases A + B + C + D

events.label: Hutchison effect corpus
events.note: Video/sample corpus; military delegation visits documented 1983; never independently reproduced under controls.

events.label: Biefeld-Brown gravitator trials
events.note: Charged asymmetric capacitors; Project Winterhaven proposal 1952.

events.label: Lifter replications and vacuum tests
events.principals: Community (Naudin et al.); NASA EHD studies
events.note: Widely replicated as EHD thrust; effect vanishes in vacuum.

events.label: Searl Effect Generator narrative
events.note: Spin-up, weight loss, halo, cooling per narrative corpus; no controlled documentation.

events.label: Roschin–Godin magnetic rotor experiment
events.note: Reported weight change, pink glow, cooling around rotating magnet assembly.

events.label: Fleischmann–Pons excess heat announcement
events.note: Pd-D electrolysis; announcement Utah, 23 March 1989.

events.label: Caltech/MIT cold-fusion rechecks
events.note: Adversarial replications reporting null within calorimetric error.

events.label: Jones low-rate fusion neutrons
events.note: Neutron rates far below F-P heat claims.

events.label: Helium–excess-heat correlation
events.note: Reported He-4 commensurate with excess heat in Pd-D cells.

events.label: Iwamura permeation transmutation findings
events.note: Cs→Pr reported after D2 permeation through Pd/CaO multilayers.

events.label: Acoustic cavitation 'bubble fusion'
events.note: Sonofusion claim; later Purdue research-conduct inquiries (2008).

events.label: Rossi E-Cat demonstrations
events.note: Ni-H reactor demos; Industrial Heat litigation settled 2017.

events.label: Google-funded LENR reinvestigation
events.note: Multi-lab program; no confirmed excess heat to date.

events.label: Rotating-superconductor weight reduction
events.note: 0.05–0.3% weight loss reported above rotating YBCO disc.

events.label: NASA MSFC gravity-shielding rebuilds
events.principals: Ning Li, Ron Koczor et al.
events.note: Partial rebuilds; no confirmed effect.

events.label: 'Impulse gravity generator' preprint
events.note: Claimed force beam from superconducting discharge; preprint only.

events.label: AC Gravity LLC claims
events.note: Announced gravity-modification research; no public results.

events.label: Right-spinning gyroscope weight loss
events.note: Chirality-dependent weight loss reported.

events.label: Gyroscope null replication
events.note: No anomaly at higher precision.

events.label: Laithwaite gyroscope lecture
events.note: Perceived gyro 'weight loss'; later resolved within classical mechanics.

events.label: Spinning ball drop experiment
events.note: Claimed altered fall/rise of rotating steel balls.

events.label: N-machine homopolar over-unity claims
events.note: Faraday-disc generator with claimed anomalous output.

events.label: Allais eclipse pendulum anomalies
events.note: Anomalous paraconical-pendulum precession during solar eclipses.

events.label: Torsion pendulum during 1970 eclipse
events.note: Period change reported during solar eclipse.

events.label: Eclipse gravimeter residuals
events.note: Small unexplained residuals during total eclipse; contested.

events.label: Kozyrev torsion/astronomical detector claims
events.note: 'Causal mechanics'; detectors reportedly responding to true star positions.

events.label: EmDrive original claims
events.note: Asymmetric resonant cavity thrust claims.

events.label: Eagleworks EmDrive vacuum measurement
events.note: ~1.2 mN/kW reported on torsion balance in vacuum.

events.label: TU Dresden SpaceDrive null results
events.note: Thrust signals traced to thermal/interaction artifacts at higher precision.

events.label: Rotating-superconductor acceleration signals
events.note: Reported acceleration fields near spun cryogenic rings; later attributed to artifacts by the group itself.

events.label: Mach-effect thruster measurements
events.note: µN-scale thrust claims on torsion balances; adversarial campaigns ongoing.

events.label: Dean Drive reactionless-thrust claims
events.note: Popularized via Astounding Science Fiction; friction-rectified vibration is the standard account.

events.label: Bessler (Orffyreus) wheel
events.note: 54-day sealed-room run, Kassel 1717, attested by officials; hidden-drive allegation by a maid.

events.label: Keely sympathetic-vibration motors
events.note: Posthumous 1899 findings of concealed compressed-air lines.

events.label: Testatika (Methernitha) machine
events.note: Influence-machine shown to visitors; independent testing declined.

events.label: Moray radiant-energy receiver
events.note: Multi-kW demonstrations reported by witnesses; device later destroyed.

events.label: Hendershot fuelless generator
events.note: Aviation-era publicity; never independently validated.

events.label: Tesla radiant-energy apparatus patent
events.note: Patent documents the claim (filing, not observation grade).

events.label: Tesla mechanical-oscillator resonance lore
events.note: Houston Street 'earthquake machine' anecdotes; resonance physics is orthodox, the magnitude lore is not.

events.label: Steorn Orbo
events.note: Economist ad 2006; failed Kinetica demo 2007; own jury 2009: no evidence of energy production.

events.label: Motionless Electromagnetic Generator
events.note: COP>1 claimed for flux-switched transformer; patent granted 2002.

events.label: Sweet Vacuum Triode Amplifier (VTA)
events.note: Conditioned-magnet device; witness accounts report self-powering, device weight loss under load, and cooling.

events.label: Lutec 1000 over-unity claims
events.note: Australian magnet-motor company; claims never independently validated.

events.label: Quantum Energy Generator (QEG)
events.note: Crowdfunded open-source builds; no build confirmed over-unity.

events.label: Kapanadze generator videos
events.note: Meter-and-lamp demonstrations; hidden-supply hypothesis repeatedly raised.

events.label: Adams pulsed magnet motor
events.note: Community-replicated as a motor; over-unity claim unconfirmed.

events.label: Bedini SSG and battery rejuvenation
events.note: Widely built pulse motor; desulfation offers a conventional account of battery 'recovery'.

events.label: Newman Energy Machine and NBS test
events.note: 1986 NBS measurement: efficiency below 100% on all tests; patent litigation followed.

events.label: Coler Magnetstromapparat
events.note: Investigated by German navy and postwar British intelligence (BIOS 2394); never reproduced.

events.label: Howard Johnson permanent-magnet motor
events.note: Patent granted for magnet-gate geometry; working self-runner never validated.

events.label: Mylow magnet-motor videos
events.note: Claimed Howard-Johnson replication; frame analysis raised a hidden-thread hypothesis.

events.label: Steven Mark Toroidal Power Unit
events.note: Videotaped toroid demos; device and inventor withdrew from scrutiny.

events.label: Gray EMA motor / 'cold electricity'
events.note: Pulsed-discharge motor; 'cold' conductor reports.

events.label: Papp noble-gas engine demo
events.note: Caltech demonstration ended in fatal explosion; Richard Feynman was present and wrote a skeptical account.

events.label: Correa PAGD reactors
events.note: Pulsed abnormal glow discharge with claimed net energy.

events.label: Chernetsky self-generating discharge
events.note: Soviet-era plasma-discharge over-unity reports.

events.label: Ehrenhaft magnetic-monopole reports
events.note: Particle motions read as 'magnetolysis'/monopoles; never confirmed.

events.label: Meyl scalar-wave transmission kits
events.note: Demonstration kits; conventional near-field coupling is the standard account.

events.label: Hooper motional-field generator
events.note: Claimed shielding-independent induced fields.

events.label: Mills hydrino / SunCell program
events.note: Below-ground-state hydrogen claims with extensive self-published data; demonstrates the X- private-extension mechanism of §5.

events.label: Montagnier DNA electromagnetic signals
events.note: Claimed EM signatures and 'teleported' DNA templates from high dilutions.

events.label: Benveniste high-dilution study
events.note: Basophil degranulation at extreme dilution.

events.label: Nature supervised recheck of Benveniste
events.principals: John Maddox, James Randi, Walter Stewart
events.note: Blinded protocol; effect not reproduced.

events.label: Emoto water-crystal photographs
events.note: Selection of crystal images correlated with intentions/labels.

events.label: Grander water vitalization
events.note: Commercialized 'informed water' devices.

events.label: Keshe GANS material claims
events.note: 'Gas in nano state' production claims.

events.label: Kervran biological transmutations
events.note: Calcium balances in hens etc.; mass-balance errors are the standard account.

events.label: Orgone accumulator ΔT and Einstein examination
events.note: Einstein reproduced a ΔT and attributed it to convection; correspondence ended there.

events.label: Rife beam-ray and universal microscope
events.note: Claimed frequency-specific devitalization of microorganisms.

events.label: Pyramid-power germination trials
events.note: Seed/razor-blade claims; controlled follow-ups null.

events.label: Grebennikov cavity-structure platform
events.note: Book-documented levitation platform based on insect-shell geometry.

events.label: Leedskalnin Coral Castle handling lore
events.note: Single-handed megalith construction; documented tripod/rigging evidence offers the standard account.

events.label: Schauberger implosion devices
events.note: Vortex 'implosion' engineering; Repulsine lore.

events.label: Hamel magnetic devices
events.note: Cone-and-magnet constructions; anecdotal flight claims.

events.label: Finsrud perpetual-motion sculpture
events.note: Publicly exhibited rolling-ball sculpture in Norway; clever mechanics is the standard account.

events.label: Otis Carr OTC-X1 craft
events.note: Free-energy saucer venture (OTC-X1, 'Utron' accumulator); a 1959 public demonstration did not take place. Convicted in Oklahoma in 1961 of selling securities without registering them — a registration offense, not a finding of fraud — and fined USD 5,000; conviction affirmed on appeal.
refs: Carr v. State, 1961 OK CR 15, 359 P.2d 606 (Okla. Crim. App., 11 Jan 1961), case no. A-12907 — conviction under 71 O.S.A. § 53 affirmed.

events.label: Meyer water fuel cell
events.note: Above-Faraday electrolysis claims sold to investors as technology dealerships; an Ohio court found fraud in 1996 and ordered repayment of USD 25,000 to two investors, after court-appointed experts reported the cell performed conventional electrolysis. Reported contemporaneously in the press; the court record itself was not consulted.
refs: Edwards, Tony. 'End of road for car that ran on water.' The Sunday Times, 1 December 1996, Features p. 12.

events.label: Garrett water-carburetor patent
events.note: Patent documents the claim; demonstration press reports exist.

events.label: Dingel water-powered car
events.note: Philippine water-car claims; the state science department assessed the device and rejected it. Convicted of estafa in 2008 by the Parañaque City Regional Trial Court over research funds taken from a Taiwanese investor and never applied to the promised prototypes — a misappropriation finding, not a verdict on the device. An appeal was announced; its outcome is not established here.
refs: Allison Lopez, 'Inventor, 82, gets 20 years for estafa', Philippine Daily Inquirer, 20 December 2008 — Paranaque City RTC Branch 257, decision of 9 December 2008, USD 380,000 in damages.

events.label: Brown's Gas anomalous-flame claims
events.note: Oxyhydrogen torches are real; 'cool flame melts tungsten' anomaly claims are the delta.

events.label: Griggs hydrosonic pump over-unity claims
events.note: Cavitation heater; heating is orthodox, COP>1 claim is not confirmed.

events.label: Pantone GEET fuel processor
events.note: 'Plasma' fuel-reformer claims; community builds, no controlled validation.

events.label: Taos Hum investigations
events.note: Persistent low-frequency hum reports; no external source identified for most hearers.

events.label: Golka ball-lightning attempts
events.note: Large Tesla-coil facility aimed at reproducing ball lightning.

events.label: Oregon Vortex / mystery-spot zones
events.note: Tilted-frame illusion geometry is the established account.

events.label: Orb photography corpus
events.note: Backscatter of flash on near-lens particles is the established account.

events.label: Crookes radiometer (migrated anomaly)
events.note: Paradigm case: 1874 open anomaly (T0), resolved via thermal transpiration (T4). Demonstrates temporal lattice migration.

events.label: Soviet/Russian torsion-field program
events.note: State-adjacent 'torsion generator' program; funding exposed ~1991; repeatedly targeted by the RAS pseudoscience commission (est. 1998, Kruglyakov).

events.label: Filimonenko 'warm fusion' installations
events.note: Soviet hydrolysis-energy claims decades before 1989; known mostly through later LENR-community retellings; program reportedly shut down in the 1960s.

events.label: Kirlian corona-discharge photography
events.note: 'Aura' imaging; corona discharge modulated by moisture/pressure is the established account. A migrated case like the radiometer.

events.label: Tolchin inertioid devices
events.note: Mechanical 'inertioids' claimed to translate without external reaction; friction rectification is the standard account; ancestor of the Dean-drive family.

events.label: 'Gravitsapa' inertial engine satellite test
events.note: Reactionless-thruster test reported aboard a Russian smallsat; publicly criticized by RAS members; no confirmed orbit change attributable to the device.

events.label: Veinik 'chronal field' experiments
events.note: Academician-thermodynamicist turned heterodox; clock-rate and weight claims framed in his own 'chronal' theory.

events.label: Marinov absolute-motion experiments
events.note: 'Coupled shutters/mirrors' apparatus claiming measurable aether drift; self-published corpus; died 1997.

events.label: Mizuno excess-heat and transmutation reports
events.note: Long-running electrolysis and glow-discharge program; book-length account published 1998; 2019 R20 reactor preprint circulated for community replication.

events.label: Arata-Zhang gas-loading demonstration
events.note: Public demonstration of D2 gas-loaded Pd nanopowder heat by a decorated materials scientist; helium claimed as product.

events.label: Minato magnetic motor
events.note: Patented pulsed magnet-wheel fans with claimed anomalous efficiency; media coverage, no independent efficiency audit published.

events.label: Seike gravity-control claims
events.note: Self-published 'ultra relativity' program; Moebius-coil devices with claimed weight and clock effects.

events.label: Schappeller 'glowing magnetism' device
events.note: 'Raumkraft' sphere device; never publicly demonstrated; survives via period accounts and later esoteric literature — the record indexes the period claim only.

events.label: Reichenbach Odic-force observations
events.note: Sensitives reporting glows and sensations from magnets/crystals in darkness; extensive self-published protocols; ancestor lineage of orgone-type claims.

events.label: Mesmer animal-magnetism practice
events.note: Baquet seances producing crises and cures; the claimed agent was a physical magnetic fluid.

events.label: Royal commission examination of animal magnetism
events.principals: Franklin, Lavoisier, Bailly, Guillotin et al.
events.note: Blinded trials at Passy attributing effects to imagination and imitation — the earliest C4 adversarial examination in this corpus.

events.label: Munich 'barn' dowsing experiments
events.note: Large screened-dowser study; authors reported a few 'skilled' outliers under double-blind conditions.

events.label: Enright reanalysis of the Munich data
events.note: Statistical reanalysis finding results consistent with chance; selection of outliers as the artifact.

events.label: Ehrenhaft subelectron charge claims
events.note: Charges below e reported in oil-drop-type experiments against Millikan; resolved via droplet physics and selection analysis; opens the promotion counter on P-EMG-SON-01.

events.label: Kowsky-Frost quartz levitation story
events.note: Widely reprinted period story of an RF-expanded, load-lifting quartz crystal; commonly assessed as a magazine hoax; indexed as a print claim.

events.label: Hubbard coil boat demonstration
events.note: Press-covered 'fuelless' coil said to drive a boat; later accounts tie the design to concealed radium energy — indexed as period claim plus conventional account.

events.label: Michelson-Morley aether-drift null
events.note: Orthodox anchor: expected drift absent; the null that reoriented physics.

events.label: Miller Mount Wilson aether-drift claims
events.note: Persistent ~small drift signal claimed across thousands of turns.

events.label: Shankland reanalysis of Miller data
events.note: Signal attributed to thermal gradients and statistical treatment.

events.label: Sagnac rotation interferometry
events.note: Real, foundational effect originally interpreted by its discoverer as aether evidence — interpretation migrated, phenomenon stayed; today the basis of ring-laser gyros.

events.label: Aspden rotor 'virtual inertia' anecdote
events.note: Report that a restarted rotor briefly required anomalously little spin-up energy after a stop; bench anecdote, never systematized.

events.label: Majorana gravitational-absorption experiments
events.note: Published series claiming tiny weight reduction of a mass screened by mercury/lead; never reproduced; modern constraints exclude the claimed magnitude.

events.label: Brush composition-dependent gravity claims
events.note: Papers claiming certain complex silicates fall with slightly different acceleration; contradicted by the Eoetvoes tradition of composition-independence.

events.label: Nipher electro-gravitation experiments
events.note: Cavendish-type setup with electrified masses; claimed modification of gravitational attraction; electrostatic force on the apparatus is the standard account.

events.label: Wallace 'kinemassic field' patents
events.note: Patented claims of a gravitational-like field from spinning odd-isotope material; patent documents the claim, no published measurements.

events.label: Kidd gyroscopic propulsion device
events.note: Twin-gyro rig with claimed net lift; TV-era coverage and university bench visits; no controlled confirmation.

events.label: Fifth-force reanalysis of Eoetvoes data
events.note: Reanalysis suggesting composition-dependent coupling; triggered a worldwide measurement campaign incl. an early claimed positive (Thieberger float experiment).

events.label: Eoet-Wash torsion-balance resolution
events.note: High-precision nulls closed the fifth-force window; local mass gradients and data treatment explained earlier signals. Model resolution arc entirely inside orthodoxy.

events.label: Patterson Power Cell demonstrations
events.note: Coated-bead electrolytic cell shown at trade conferences with high claimed COP; kits circulated; never independently validated or commercialized.

events.label: Klein HHO/Aquygen media wave
events.note: TV-covered torch and car demonstrations under a branded oxyhydrogen; conventional combustion accounts discussed.

events.label: Kanarev low-current electrolysis claims
events.note: Pulsed 'plasma electrolysis' with claimed over-unity hydrogen yield; self-published monographs.

events.label: Santilli magnecule gas claims
events.note: Arc-gasification fuel marketed with claimed novel 'magnecule' species; conventional syngas chemistry is the discussed account.

events.label: Puharich resonant water-splitting patent
events.note: Patented claims of efficient resonant water dissociation; patent documents the claim.

events.label: Abrams ERA radionics practice
events.note: 'Electronic Reactions of Abrams': diagnosis from blood spots via box instruments; large practitioner network in the early 1920s.

events.label: Scientific American ERA investigation
events.note: Year-long blinded examination concluding the devices had no diagnostic value.

events.label: Drown radio-vision instruments
events.note: Claimed photographic imaging and treatment of distant patients from a blood sample. A 1949 University of Chicago test of the diagnostic device failed: three of the first blood specimens were so badly misread that the remaining seven were not attempted. Convicted in 1951 of introducing a misbranded device into interstate commerce and fined USD 1,000 — a labelling offence under food and drug law, not a fraud finding. A 1963 California grand-theft charge did not come to trial; she died in 1965.
refs: Ralph Lee Smith, 'The Incredible Drown Case', Today's Health (American Medical Association), April 1968 — reproduced at quackwatch.org/chiropractic/hx/drown/.

events.label: de la Warr radionic camera
events.note: UK radionics laboratory producing 'thought photographs'; 1960 civil litigation ended without a fraud finding.

events.label: Hieronymus eloptic-energy analyzer
events.note: Patented 'emanation' analyzer; Astounding editor J.W. Campbell reported symbolic circuit-diagram versions working equally well — inadvertently a suggestion-artifact experiment.

events.label: Lakhovsky Multiple Wave Oscillator trials
events.note: Plant-tumor and clinical claims around broadband spark-gap coils; hospital-adjacent trials reported in period sources.

events.label: Priore plasma-tube therapy machine
events.note: Large undisclosed-principle machine; animal-trial cures reported with Academy-level advocacy and state funding; device principle never disclosed, program collapsed.

events.label: Perendev magnet motor venture
events.note: Video-marketed all-magnet motor with investment scheme; the venture was the subject of subsequent legal proceedings. No court outcome is asserted here — none could be verified against a primary source.

events.label: Yildiz magnet motor demonstration
events.note: University-venue demonstration (Delft, 2010) of a self-running magnet motor; teardown under controls never granted.

events.label: Heins 'Perepiteia' regenerative claims
events.note: Motor acceleration under load claimed; press-reported bench visits at university labs; conventional back-EMF/field-weakening accounts discussed.

events.label: Bhaskara overbalanced wheel description
events.note: Earliest known perpetual-wheel description (mercury-filled spokes); the deep-history root of S-SLF; Villard de Honnecourt's 13th-c. sketch continues the lineage.

events.label: Cox's barometric perpetual clock
events.note: Orthodox anchor: an apparent self-runner with a fully documented energy source (barometric pressure); the control case every S-SLF claim is measured against.

events.label: Blondlot N-rays
events.note: New radiation reported via faint screen-brightness changes; dozens of confirming papers within France in one year — a replication bubble.

events.label: Wood's examination of N-rays
events.note: Covert prism removal left reported observations unchanged; the canonical observer-expectancy exposure.

events.label: Polywater / anomalous water
events.note: Capillary-condensed water with anomalous properties; a global replication wave followed.

events.label: Polywater contamination resolution
events.note: Silicate/organic contamination identified; original group conceded — a clean self-correction arc.

events.label: Mpemba effect
events.note: Hot-water-freezes-faster reports; multiple conventional mechanisms discussed (evaporation, convection, supercooling, definitions), none settled — a live orthodox-sector anomaly.

events.label: Pioneer anomalous acceleration
events.note: Sunward ~1e-9 m/s^2 residual on both spacecraft; open anomaly for 14 years.

events.label: Pioneer thermal-recoil resolution
events.note: Anisotropic thermal radiation recoil accounts for the acceleration — a migrated anomaly, radiometer-style, at spacecraft scale.

events.label: OPERA superluminal-neutrino announcement
events.note: 60 ns early neutrino arrival reported with explicit request for scrutiny.

events.label: OPERA fiber/clock artifact resolution
events.note: Loose fiber connector and clock drift identified; ICARUS null; textbook fast self-correction.

events.label: NASA lattice-confinement fusion
events.note: Gamma-driven deuteron fusion in metal lattices published in a mainstream venue — orthodox-adjacent boundary case for the S-LNR neighborhood.

events.label: Piantelli-Focardi Ni-H anomalous heat
events.note: Nickel-hydrogen excess-heat series published in Il Nuovo Cimento (1994); the direct ancestor of the E-Cat lineage.

events.label: SPAWAR co-deposition CR-39 tracks
events.note: Pd co-deposition with track-detector pits read as energetic particles; chemical-damage accounts discussed.

events.label: Peregrinus perpetual magnetic wheel design
events.note: Epistola de magnete closes with a lodestone-driven wheel intended to turn perpetually — the root document of the magnet-motor lineage, seven centuries before Perendev.

events.label: Villard de Honnecourt overbalanced wheel
events.note: Sketchbook wheel with hinged hammers, asserted to run of itself — the European entry of the overbalanced-wheel idea.

events.label: Zimara self-blowing windmill proposal
events.note: Windmill driving bellows that blow the windmill — the closed-loop archetype in its purest form.

events.label: Fludd closed-cycle water mill
events.note: Recirculating water-screw mill grinding grain from its own outflow; endlessly reprinted ancestor of hydraulic self-runner claims.

events.label: Leonardo's refutation of overbalanced wheels
events.note: Notebook analyses working the torque balance and dismissing the seekers of perpetual motion — the earliest analysis-type resolution record in the corpus.

events.label: Drebbel perpetuum mobile at the Stuart court
events.note: Celebrated self-moving globe presented to James I; modern reading: barometric/thermal drive — Cox's clock avant la lettre, an apparent self-runner with a real energy source.

events.label: Marquess of Worcester wheel demonstration
events.note: Large weighted wheel reportedly shown before the king at the Tower of London; known through the inventor's Century of Inventions and court lore.

events.label: Sepulchral ever-burning lamp reports
events.principals: Renaissance antiquarian tradition (compiled by Fortunio Liceti)
events.note: Recurring reports of lamps found alight in opened ancient tombs (the Tulliola account among them); the medieval-to-early-modern free-energy claim par excellence, surviving only as secondary reports.

events.label: Redheffer perpetual motion machine
events.note: Paid-admission perpetual wheel; a Philadelphia inspection already noticed gear wear running the wrong way.

events.label: Fulton's exposure of the Redheffer machine
events.note: Fulton traced an uneven running rhythm to a concealed catgut belt and an elderly man cranking in an upper room — the template hoax exposure, ninety years before Wood's prism.

events.label: Garabed free-energy claim before the US Congress
events.note: Congress passed a joint resolution to examine the 'Garabed'; the committee identified it as a flywheel storing, not generating, energy — a legislative-branch P4 record.

events.label: Cook electro-magnetic self-acting battery
events.note: Community-flagged instance. Patented coil pair claimed to sustain its own current — the earliest US patent document in the electromagnetic self-runner lineage; compiler's recall dates it 1871, not 1895.

events.label: Gary neutral-line magnetic motor
events.note: 'Neutral line' magnet motor with sympathetic national-press coverage; the 19th-century prototype of the magnet-motor media wave.

events.label: Pogue high-mileage vapor carburetor
events.note: Patented vapor carburetors with ~200-mpg claims; never validated under controls; seed of the suppressed-carburetor narrative (nar:pogue-200mpg).

events.label: Tewari reactionless generator claims
events.note: Ex-nuclear-engineering executive; over-unity homopolar-style generator claims framed by his space-vortex theory (doc:tewari-space-vortex).

events.label: Haramein holofractographic unified theory
events.summary: Schwarzschild-proton and vacuum-geometry papers proposing a unified field; presented largely outside peer-reviewed venues.
events.note: Schwarzschild-proton and vacuum-geometry papers proposing a unified field; presented largely outside peer-reviewed venues.

events.label: Heim multidimensional field theory
events.summary: Six/eight-dimensional structured field theory with a claimed particle-mass formula; propulsion implications discussed posthumously.
events.note: Six/eight-dimensional structured field theory with a claimed particle-mass formula; propulsion implications discussed posthumously.

events.label: Shipov physical-vacuum / torsion theory
events.summary: Theory of the physical vacuum underpinning the torsion-field program.
events.note: Theory of the physical vacuum underpinning the torsion-field program.

events.label: Bearden scalar electromagnetics
events.summary: Scalar-wave and vacuum-engineering corpus (Energy from the Vacuum) framing MEG-type devices.
events.note: Scalar-wave and vacuum-engineering corpus (Energy from the Vacuum) framing MEG-type devices.

events.label: Mills Grand Unified Theory of Classical Physics
events.summary: Classical-physics reformulation predicting hydrino states below the hydrogen ground state.
events.note: Classical-physics reformulation predicting hydrino states below the hydrogen ground state.

events.label: Larson Reciprocal System
events.summary: Motion-as-fundamental cosmology with a persistent study community.
events.note: Motion-as-fundamental cosmology with a persistent study community.

events.label: LaViolette subquantum kinetics
events.summary: Reaction-diffusion ether model; author also popularized electrogravitics-in-aerospace claims.
events.note: Reaction-diffusion ether model; author also popularized electrogravitics-in-aerospace claims.

events.label: Correa aetherometry
events.summary: Massfree-energy framework built atop the PAGD experimental line.
events.note: Massfree-energy framework built atop the PAGD experimental line.

events.label: Hoerbiger World Ice Theory
events.summary: Cosmic-ice cosmology with mass following in interwar Central Europe.
events.note: Cosmic-ice cosmology with mass following in interwar Central Europe.

events.label: Tewari space vortex theory
events.summary: Vacuum-vortex model of the electron motivating reactionless-generator work.
events.note: Vacuum-vortex model of the electron motivating reactionless-generator work.

events.label: Philadelphia Experiment
events.emergence: 1955 (Allende letters to M. K. Jessup); book wave 1979
events.narrative_provenance: Carl Allen/'Carlos Allende' correspondence and annotated Jessup volume; Berlitz & Moore popularization; ONR public denials.
events.factual_kernel: USS Eldridge existed; wartime degaussing research against magnetic mines was real.
events.lore_layer: Optical invisibility, teleportation to Norfolk, fused crewmen.
events.note: Kernel: USS Eldridge existed; wartime degaussing research against magnetic mines was real. / Lore: Optical invisibility, teleportation to Norfolk, fused crewmen.
events.years_raw: 1955 (Allende letters to M. K. Jessup); book wave 1979

events.label: Die Glocke (Nazi bell device)
events.narrative_provenance: Claimed unpublished interrogation sources cited by Witkowski; no primary-period documents produced.
events.factual_kernel: SS technical programs and the Wenceslas-mine area exist.
events.lore_layer: Bell-shaped antigravity/time device, Xerum-525, Kecksburg tie-in.
events.note: Kernel: SS technical programs and the Wenceslas-mine area exist. / Lore: Bell-shaped antigravity/time device, Xerum-525, Kecksburg tie-in.

events.label: Nazi-UFO / Vril complex
events.narrative_provenance: Postwar individual claims of saucer projects; Pauwels/Bergier and later channelled 'Vril Society' literature.
events.factual_kernel: Advanced conventional German aeronautics existed; the 'Vril Society' itself lacks primary-period evidence.
events.lore_layer: Flight discs, Thule/Vril occult engineering, Antarctic bases.
events.note: Kernel: Advanced conventional German aeronautics existed; the 'Vril Society' itself lacks primary-period evidence. / Lore: Flight discs, Thule/Vril occult engineering, Antarctic bases.

events.label: Tesla suppressed-papers narrative
events.emergence: from 1943; recurrent waves with FOIA releases
events.narrative_provenance: Office of Alien Property custody of Tesla's effects; public FBI file; John G. Trump's review memorandum.
events.factual_kernel: Custody of the estate and a government review are documented; the review found nothing operational.
events.lore_layer: Hidden death-ray and free-energy technology withheld from humanity.
events.note: Kernel: Custody of the estate and a government review are documented; the review found nothing operational. / Lore: Hidden death-ray and free-energy technology withheld from humanity.
events.years_raw: from 1943; recurrent waves with FOIA releases

events.label: Suppressed-inventor master narrative
events.narrative_provenance: Retold across the free-energy literature; each generation adds its martyr.
events.factual_kernel: Moray reported attacks on his lab; Meyer died suddenly in 1998 with death officially attributed to natural causes; several inventors faced real legal action (documented in the respective records).
events.lore_layer: A coordinated cartel systematically silencing working devices.
events.note: Kernel: Moray reported attacks on his lab; Meyer died suddenly in 1998 with death officially attributed to natural causes; several inventors faced real legal action (documented in the respective records). / Lore: A coordinated cartel systematically silencing working devices.

events.label: Suppressed 200-mpg carburetor
events.narrative_provenance: Newspaper claims around Pogue's devices; oral automotive folklore.
events.factual_kernel: Pogue's patents and mileage claims are documented.
events.lore_layer: Oil interests bought and buried the device.
events.note: Kernel: Pogue's patents and mileage claims are documented. / Lore: Oil interests bought and buried the device.

events.label: HAARP weather/mind-control claims
events.narrative_provenance: Activist books and broadcast culture around the Gakona facility.
events.factual_kernel: HAARP is a real ionospheric research heater, now university-operated, with published science.
events.lore_layer: Weather weaponization, earthquake induction, mass mind control.
events.note: Kernel: HAARP is a real ionospheric research heater, now university-operated, with published science. / Lore: Weather weaponization, earthquake induction, mass mind control.

events.label: Hutchison equipment-seizure lore
events.factual_kernel: Hutchison reports confiscations of his apparatus; delegation interest in the 1980s is documented.
events.lore_layer: State suppression of a working effect.
events.note: Kernel: Hutchison reports confiscations of his apparatus; delegation interest in the 1980s is documented. / Lore: State suppression of a working effect.

events.label: Montauk Project
events.narrative_provenance: Book-driven; derivative continuation of the Philadelphia narrative.
events.lore_layer: Time tunnels, psychic chair, child experiments.
events.note: Kernel: Camp Hero radar station exists. / Lore: Time tunnels, psychic chair, child experiments.

events.label: Free-energy patent-suppression narrative
events.narrative_provenance: Community readings of the US Invention Secrecy Act and annual secrecy-order counts.
events.factual_kernel: The Invention Secrecy Act (1951) and standing secrecy orders are real and documented via FOIA; the USPTO's historical working-model demand for perpetual-motion filings is real.
events.lore_layer: Systematic burial of functioning energy patents.
events.note: Kernel: The Invention Secrecy Act (1951) and standing secrecy orders are real and documented via FOIA; the USPTO's historical working-model demand for perpetual-motion filings is real. / Lore: Systematic burial of functioning energy patents.

events.label: Shoulders charge-cluster (EV/EVO) bench corpus
events.note: Micron-scale high-charge-density electron clusters from pulsed sharp cathodes; borehole witness marks and energy-gain claims; conventional account discussed: explosive electron emission / cathode-spot ectons (Mesyats).

events.label: Natural ball lightning observation corpus
events.note: Accepted-as-real phenomenon with open mechanism; candidate conventional theories (silicon combustion, microwave cavities) discussed, none settled.

events.label: Accidental ball-lightning spectrum (Lanzhou)
events.note: Slit-spectrograph capture during a storm showing soil-element lines (Si, Fe, Ca) — consistent with soil-vaporization accounts.

events.label: Gatchina water-discharge plasmoids
events.note: Long-lived luminous plasmoids launched from water-surface discharges; reproducible laboratory analog with sub-second lifetimes.

events.label: EVO interpretive framework
events.principals: Kenneth R. Shoulders (origin); MFMP / Bob Greenyer (contemporary school)
events.summary: Reads discharge and LENR witness marks (boreholes, micro-craters, strike patterns) as signatures of exotic vacuum objects / charge clusters; contemporary open-science analyses extend the reading across the heterodox corpus.
events.note: Reads discharge and LENR witness marks (boreholes, micro-craters, strike patterns) as signatures of exotic vacuum objects / charge clusters; contemporary open-science analyses extend the reading across the heterodox corpus.
